title: Structured Retrieval, Knowledge Graphs, GraphRAG, and Multi-Hop Evidence · Behavior Case: Rag Graphrag Community Search
summary: A team converts every document into a generated graph, loses edge provenance, uses community summaries as facts, and never compares the result with SQL or baseline retrieval. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the graphrag community search increment in provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever. Learners progres…
objectives: Explain GraphRAG indexing, communities, summaries, local, global, DRIFT, and basic search with cost, prompt-tuning, and migration limits.
Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-structured-graph-rag-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-structured-graph-rag-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_structured_graph_rag_5(entities: int = 12, relations: int = 18, hops: int = 2, provenance_edges: int = 18, schema_valid: bool = True, baseline_beaten: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if entities <= 0 or relations < 0:
        return False, 0, "graph-shape"
    if hops < 1 or hops > 4:
        return False, hops, "hop-budget"
    if provenance_edges < relations:
        return False, provenance_edges, "edge-provenance"
    if not schema_valid:
        return False, relations, "structured-contract"
    if not baseline_beaten:
        return False, relations, "complexity-not-earned"
    assert provenance_edges >= relations
    return True, hops, "grounded-multihop"

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Rag Graphrag Community Search, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the rag graphrag community search defect evidence boundary.

For repair the rag graphrag community search defect, run one convenient Rag Graphrag Community Search query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Graphrag Community Search model · Theory Rag Graphrag Community Search
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Graphrag Community Search: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 9331 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to select structured, SQL, knowledge-graph, or GraphRAG retrieval only when multi-hop evidence beats baseline; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain GraphRAG indexing, communities, summaries, local, global, DRIFT, and basic search with cost, prompt-tuning, and migration limits.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Graphrag Community Search model · Theory Rag Graphrag Community Search.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Graphrag Community Search model · Theory Rag Graphrag Community Search, keep Rag Graphrag Community Search connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Explain GraphRAG indexing, communities, summaries, local, global, DRIFT, and basic search with cost, prompt-tuning, and migration limits.

Competency probe: Explain GraphRAG indexing, communities, summaries, local, global, DRIFT, and basic search with cost, prompt-tuning, and migration limits.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Graphrag Community Search model · Theory Rag Graphrag Community Search, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: GraphRAG is a drop-in improvement over chunk retrieval for every question.
